summary: Escape `%`/`_`/`\` in `email-domain=` LIKE pattern with `ESCAPE '\'`; surface `loadProgress` errors instead of dereferencing nil progress pointer.
reason: Greptile P1 findings: (1) the unescaped LIKE pattern from a user-supplied domain like `exa%le.com` widens the deletion set to every contact whose email contains `exa…le.com`; (2) when the progress file exists but is unreadable (e.g., permission denied), `loadProgress` returns `(nil, nil, err)` and the previous caller discarded the error then dereferenced the nil `*bulkDeleteProgress` — guaranteed panic on resumed runs.
